note: Patch Manager (port 3001) was offline. Review captured Agent web UI at port 3105 instead.

findings.observation: CPU percentage displays as '7953642733%' at 1024x768 and '429496724000%' at 768x1024. Classic 32-bit integer overflow (2^32 = 4294967296). The CPU sampling code is passing raw ticks/nanoseconds to the formatter instead of a normalized 0–100 float.
findings.suggestion: Compute CPU as float64 (delta_busy/delta_total)*100, clamp to [0,100], format as integer %. Both header stat and Resources card must use the same derived value. Add a unit test asserting header == Resources for any payload.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Percentages, Numbers; enterprise.md > Professional Appearance

findings.observation: Navigating to an unknown route renders the unstyled React Router default: 'Unexpected Application Error! 404 Not Found'. Raw framework error chrome exposed to users. No branding, no navigation, no recovery action.
findings.suggestion: Add a global errorElement to the router plus a path:'*' catch-all rendering a branded 404 page (logo, 'Page not found', 'Back to Overview' button). Also add redirect from /dashboard → /.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Professional Appearance, Error Messages; interaction.md > Loading States

findings.element: Dashboard main content grid at 768px viewport
findings.observation: At 768x1024 the two-column card grid compresses into a narrow strip on the left half. System Info (OS Family/Version/Hostname) text overlaps. Top status strip is nearly unreadable. Layout does not degrade gracefully — it breaks.
findings.suggestion: Define breakpoints: below 1024px collapse to single-column full-width grid. Top stat strip wraps to 2×3 at <1280px. System Info stacks vertically at <768px. All text uses break-words.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Alignment, Visual Weight and Whitespace; ux-designer.md > Responsive behavior

findings.element: Dashboard header CPU stat vs Resources card vs Hardware page
findings.observation: In 00-initial.png header shows CPU 0%, Resources shows 0% (consistent). In 02-hover-stat.png header shows 100%, Resources shows 100% (consistent). In responsive-1024.png header overflows to 7953642733% but Resources shows 100% — same metric, two values rendered simultaneously. Dashboard contradicts itself. Additionally, 0% on an active Mac is implausible and Hardware page shows 100% at the same time.
findings.suggestion: Single source of truth: both widgets subscribe to the same CPU value from one hook. Add an E2E assertion that header CPU === Resources CPU for any API payload. Smooth with a rolling 3-sample average to avoid sudden 0→100 jumps.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals, Loading and Stale Data; consistency.md > Data Formatting

findings.observation: Every route (Overview, Patches, Hardware, Software, Services, History, Logs) lacks an h1. Confirmed by automated assertion A-001 (0 h1 elements). Only Settings has a visible page title. Screen readers and WCAG 2.1 AA audits fail without h1. Inconsistent across routes.
findings.suggestion: Add <h1> to every route via a shared PageHeader component. Examples: 'Overview', 'Pending Patches', 'Hardware', etc. Hostname stays as subtitle, not the title.
findings.standard_violated: accessibility.md > Heading Hierarchy; consistency.md > Page Layout; visual.md > Typography

findings.observation: Compliance card occupies half a row of prime dashboard space to display: 'Compliance scores are managed by your Patch Manager. Check your PM dashboard for compliance status.' No data, no link, no CTA. An anti-empty-state: text where a component should be.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) fetch and display last-cached compliance score from PM with stale indicator and deep link button 'Open Patch Manager', or (b) remove the card entirely and use the space for something actionable (Recent Activity).
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; visual.md > Visual Weight and Whitespace

findings.observation: Version displays 'dev'. For an enterprise security product, 'dev' tells the buyer: this is an unversioned dev build on my production endpoint. Procurement cannot track it in a software inventory. Support cannot correlate it with a release.
findings.suggestion: Inject semver at compile time (e.g. -ldflags '-X version=1.0.0-rc.2'). Display as '1.0.0-rc.2 (abc1234)'. Add an About section in Settings showing version, build date, build hash.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Version and Changelog, Professional Appearance

findings.element: Left sidebar — collapsed icon rail tooltips
findings.observation: In 03-patches-page.png, 07-history-page.png, 09-settings-page.png the route tooltip ('Patches', 'History', 'Settings') floats far into the page content area, overlapping the first stat card, rather than sitting adjacent to the sidebar icon. Looks like a portal positioning bug.
findings.suggestion: Set Radix Tooltip side='right' with 8px offset anchored to the sidebar icon. Use a portal with z-index above page content. Hide tooltip on route change.
findings.standard_violated: interaction.md > Hover States; visual.md > Alignment

findings.observation: The sidebar is an icon-only rail. Eight monochrome glyphs with no visible labels. Enterprise.md explicitly warns against icon-only buttons. Users navigating quickly cannot distinguish Hardware from Software from Services at a glance.
findings.suggestion: Add a labeled rail (icon + small text label) by default, or an expandable rail that shows labels on hover/expand. Each nav item must have aria-label. Consider a sticky-expand toggle.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Icons; accessibility.md > Images and Icons; enterprise.md > Localization Readiness

findings.element: Resources → CPU Usage bar + top 'Healthy' pill
findings.observation: When CPU is at 100% the Resources bar turns solid red (correct). But the top-strip overall status remains 'Healthy' (green). Product simultaneously signals 'Healthy' and 'CPU on fire'. Color is the only differentiator — no paired text label ('Critical', '100% — High load').
findings.suggestion: Define a health state machine: Healthy → Degraded → Critical driven by resource thresholds. Propagate CPU criticality into the global health pill. Pair color with text label on the bar ('100% — Critical'). Never rely on color alone.
findings.standard_violated: accessibility.md > Color as the Sole Differentiator; data-integrity.md > Status Indicators; consistency.md > Color Meaning

findings.observation: Six stat pills (Healthy | CPU | Memory | Disk | Network | Patches) crammed with pipe separators and no breathing room at 1024px. Even with correct CPU values the layout has zero padding between items.
findings.suggestion: Below 1280px switch from pipe-separator strip to a responsive stat card row with min-width per item, wrap behavior, 16px horizontal padding, 12px gap.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Spacing, Visual Weight and Whitespace

findings.element: Agent ID in Agent Health card
findings.observation: Agent ID displayed as truncated UUID 'd4a49fb8…' with copy icon. UUID is the only identifier — no hostname-based primary label. Enterprise.md forbids raw UUIDs as primary identifiers.
findings.suggestion: Use hostname as primary Agent label. Move Agent ID to a 'Diagnostics' collapsible in Settings as a copyable technical field labeled 'Agent ID (UUID)'.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Professional Appearance; data-integrity.md > Long Text

findings.element: Hardware > System Load + Resources CPU core count
findings.observation: System Load shows '1 cores' on what is an 8-core MacBook Air. Load bars are red at 1.61 (over-capacity) because 1 core is the denominator. This is wrong — the agent is failing CPU topology detection. 'CPU 0%' on the dashboard, 'CPU 100%' on Hardware, '1 core', overflow % — the entire CPU subsystem is unreliable.
findings.suggestion: Fix CPU topology: use runtime.NumCPU() in Go or sysctl -n hw.logicalcpu on darwin. Recompute load thresholds against actual core count. Fix plural: '${n} core${n===1?'':'s'}'.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Numbers, Charts; consistency.md > Data Formatting

findings.observation: Hardware page says 'Auto-refresh 3s' but the Overview shows no freshness indicator on any card. Users cannot tell if CPU 0% is current or stale.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Updated Xs ago' footer to each live card on Overview, matching the Hardware page convention. Add a manual refresh button.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Loading and Stale Data

findings.observation: favicon.ico returns 404. Console error on every page load.
findings.suggestion: Add favicon.ico + favicon.svg to public/ and reference in index.html.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Branding

findings.observation: All log entries look identical — monotonic 'Heartbeat sent to server' lines with no severity color coding, no grouping/collapse for repeated messages.
findings.suggestion: Color rows by level (info=default, warn=amber, error=red). Group consecutive identical messages with a count badge. Default filter to warn+ unless debug opted in.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Status Indicators; visual.md > Visual Weight and Whitespace

findings.observation: Form controls (Heartbeat Interval, Bandwidth Limit, etc.) are visible with no Save/Apply button in view. No feedback whether changes auto-save or require explicit commit.
findings.suggestion: Add a sticky 'Save Changes' button footer that appears only when a field is dirty. Show success toast on save. Disable button when no changes.
findings.standard_violated: consistency.md > Form Patterns; interaction.md > Loading States

findings.observation: Lists raw BSD interface names (gif0*, stf0*, anpi1, anpi0) all showing 0 B/s. Asterisks unexplained. Dumps every kernel pseudo-interface.
findings.suggestion: Filter to active interfaces with non-zero throughput by default. Add 'Show inactive' toggle. Add interface type labels (Tunnel, Loopback, Wi-Fi). Add asterisk legend or remove.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Empty States; enterprise.md > Scale Behavior

findings.element: Relative timestamps (Server Connection > Last Heartbeat, etc.)
findings.observation: Shows '16s ago' / '21s ago' with no tooltip for absolute time. Critical for log correlation and timezone-aware debugging.
findings.suggestion: Wrap every relative timestamp in a tooltip showing absolute ISO 8601 + local TZ: '2026-04-08 14:32:18 UTC / 10:32 AM local'.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Dates and Times

findings.observation: Avatar labeled 'Agent / Local device' with 'AG' initials sits in the user-avatar slot. The visual grammar implies sign-in identity. The agent UI has no real user. Confusing identity model.
findings.suggestion: Either remove the avatar slot (this is an unattended host-local service), or replace with a clear 'Local Agent' system badge that does not mimic user account patterns from web/web-hub.
findings.standard_violated: consistency.md > Terminology; enterprise.md > Professional Appearance

findings.observation: OS Family / OS Version / Hostname appear as a bare row of labeled values without card chrome, unlike every other section which uses a card container.
findings.suggestion: Wrap in a Card with title 'System Information' and 3-column key-value grid, matching Agent Health and Server Connection card visual weight.
findings.standard_violated: consistency.md > Card Patterns; visual.md > Alignment

findings.observation: 490 com.apple.* launchd labels with no curation or actionability. Default view should surface only patch-relevant services.
findings.suggestion: Default to 'PatchIQ-managed services' only with a 'Show all system services' toggle. Or remove from primary nav.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Scale Behavior; data-integrity.md > Empty States

findings.observation: Hostname 'Shandeshs-MacBook-Air.local' displayed in monospace/console font inconsistent with rest of the sans-serif UI. No 'Endpoint:' label. Reads as a debug breadcrumb.
findings.suggestion: Use body sans-serif. Prepend 'ENDPOINT' label or icon. Show short hostname with FQDN in tooltip.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Typography; consistency.md > Naming Conventions

findings.observation: Page opens with a gray description paragraph 'Patch deployment history — records of...' with no h1 above it. Inverted heading hierarchy — description without title.
findings.suggestion:
Add

History

above the description using the same PageHeader component as Settings.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Typography; consistency.md > Page Layout

findings.observation: Shows '↑ 0 B/s + 0 B/s' — plus sign used as RX/TX separator reads as arithmetic addition. Other screenshots show up/down arrows (inconsistent).
findings.suggestion: Use '↓ 641.7 KB/s • ↑ 18.2 KB/s' with bullet separator, consistent units, distinct direction arrows.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Numbers; consistency.md > Data Formatting

findings.observation: First stat card 'TOTAL PENDING' shows persistent green border (selected state?) not a transient focus ring. '0' numerals use slashed-zero font glyph reading as 'ø'. Tooltip 'Patches' floats over first card.
findings.suggestion: Remove persistent border on first card (or clarify it as active filter state using background tint, not border). Disable slashed-zero with font-feature-settings: 'zero' 0. Fix tooltip positioning (see PO-008).
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Spacing; interaction.md > Focus States; data-integrity.md > Zero vs Null

findings.element: Software page — claude-code listed as managed software
findings.observation: Software inventory shows claude-code 2.1.85 (184.3MB) as a 'managed' package. Enterprise buyers will ask uncomfortable questions about Anthropic tooling being managed by their patch system.
findings.suggestion: Provide clear definition of 'managed' (which policy engine). Categorize developer tooling separately. Consider a filter to exclude dev tools from the primary managed view.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Professional Appearance

findings.element: Services page — no 'Showing N of 490' indicator on filtered view
findings.observation: When filter tabs are active the table reduces rows but no 'Showing X of 490 services' count is visible.
findings.suggestion: Add 'Showing N of 490 services' footer that reflects the active filter at all times.
findings.standard_violated: data-integrity.md > Counts and Totals

findings.observation: Sun icon with no visible label, no tooltip text. Icon-only control requires aria-label and tooltip per accessibility + interaction standards.
findings.suggestion: Add aria-label='Toggle theme' and tooltip 'Switch to dark mode' / 'Switch to light mode'.
findings.standard_violated: accessibility.md > Images and Icons; interaction.md > Hover States

findings.element: Logs page log viewer column width
findings.observation: Log viewer appears narrower than the full content area. For a log viewer, horizontal space directly correlates with readability of long lines.
findings.suggestion: Let the logs area fill 100% available width. Use monospace font 12–13px with horizontal scroll for extreme lines.
findings.standard_violated: ux-designer.md > Information density

findings.element: No tenant indicator in Agent UI
findings.observation: Agent UI shows hostname but not which tenant/PM instance this endpoint is enrolled to. MSP/multi-tenant buyers need this context.
findings.suggestion: Add a small 'PM: ssh.skenzer.com' or tenant name badge in Server Connection card or top bar alongside hostname.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Multi-Tenant Isolation

findings.element: Theme toggle — light mode parity unverified
findings.observation: Theme toggle exists but every screenshot is dark mode. Cannot verify light mode is fully implemented.
findings.suggestion: Either verify all components are themed in light mode or hide the toggle until light mode is production-ready.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Dark and Light Mode

findings.observation: PatchIQ shield logo is a generic shadcn-style outline. Not distinctive enough for a $500K enterprise product.
findings.suggestion: Commission a proper logo with distinguishing mark. At minimum a custom shield with subtle gradient or wordmark treatment.
findings.standard_violated: enterprise.md > Branding

findings.observation: SOURCE/CATEGORY filter rows stacked with inconsistent vertical gap. Buttons run edge-to-edge.
findings.suggestion: Add 16px vertical gap between filter rows. Align labels left consistently. Use FilterBar pattern from other list pages.
findings.standard_violated: visual.md > Spacing; consistency.md > Page Layout

business_rule_results.rule: Business rules were generated for Patch Manager dashboard (port 3001) which was offline. Rules below are from Agent UI exploration instead.
business_rule_results.details: Patch Manager server not running — rules could not be verified.
